LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF FUSIONSTORM'S MOTION FOR A TEMPORARY PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT RENEE TENORIO IS ENJOINED DURING THE PENDENCY OF THIS LAWSUIT FROM USING, DISCLOSING, OR DISSEMINATING ANY INFORMATION PERTAINING TO FUSIONSTORM'S BUSINESS WHICH SHE TRANSFERRED OFF OF HER FUSIONSTORM-ISSUED COMPUTER WITHOUT THE PERMISSION OF AN AUTHORIZED REPRESENTATIVE OF FUSIONSTORM. IN DEPT. 302; OPPOSITION TO BE FILED BY DECEMBER 23, 2016. COURTESY COPIES TO BE DELIVERED TO DEPT. 302 ON ALL PAPERS FILED WITH THE COURT. LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF FUSIONSTORM'S MOTION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT RENEE TENORIO IS ENJOINED, PENDING THE ENTRY OF JUDGMENT IN THIS CASE, FROM DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY USING, DISCLOSING OR DISSEMINATING ANY FILES, INFORMATION OR DATA TAKEN FROM FUSIONSTORM. LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT FUSIONSTORM'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AFTER JUDGMENT IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH FUSIONSTORM HAS NEITHER WAIVED NOR IS JUDICIALLY ESTOPPED FROM SEEKING ATTORNEY'S FEES, IT FAILS TO IDENTIFY A BASIS TO OBTAIN A FEES AWARD. A PREVAILING DEFENDANT ON A FEHA CLAIM IS NOT ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF FEES UNLESS "UNLESS THE COURT FINDS THE ACTION WAS OBJECTIVELY WITHOUT FOUNDATION WHEN BROUGHT, OR THE PLAINTIFF CONTINUED TO LITIGATE AFTER IT CLEARLY BECAME SO." (WILLIAMS V. CHINO VALLEY INDEPENDENT FIRE DISTRICT (2015) 61 CAL. 4TH 97, 115). THE COURT'S OWN REVIEW OF THE FILE IN THIS CASE, INCLUDING THE PAPERS FILED ON THIS MOTION, IS INSUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT A FINDING THAT MS. TENORIO'S FEHA CLAIMS WERE OBJECTIVELY WITHOUT FOUNDATION WHEN BROUGHT OR SHE CONTINUED TO LITIGATE THEM AFTER THEY CLEARLY BECAME SO. NOR IS FUSIONSTORM ENTITLED TO FEES PER THE FEES PROVISION IN MS. TENORIO'S SEVERANCE AGREEMENT BECAUSE, BY THIS LAWSUIT, FUSIONSTORM DID NOT RECOVER ANY CONSIDERATION PAID TO MS. TENORIO PER THAT AGREEMENT, WHICH IS THE ONLY BASIS FOR AN AWARD OF CONTRACTUAL FEES RELIED ON BY FUSIONSTORM. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE; HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/HK

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF FUSIONSTORM'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH FUSIONSTORM HAS NEITHER WAIVED NOR IS JUDICIALLY ESTOPPED FROM SEEKING ATTORNEY'S FEES AND IT OBTAINED INJUNCTIVE RELIEF ON ITS PENAL CODE 502(C) CLAIM, THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION PER PENAL CODE 502(E)(1) NOT TO AWARD ANY FEES TO FUSIONSTORM. FUSIONSTORM NEVER PROVIDED ANY PERSUASIVE EVIDENCE THAT MS. TENORIO MISUSED ANY OF FUSIONSTORM'S INFORMATION IN A WAY THAT CAUSED ANY HARM. MOREOVER, THE INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IT SOUGHT AND OBTAINED WAS MORE PROPHYLACTIC THAN REMEDIAL. JUDGE; HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/HK

Motion For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Oct-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff FusionStorm's motion for attorneys' fees is denied. Although FusionStorm has neither waived nor is judicially estopped from seeking attorney's fees and it obtained injunctive relief on its Penal Code 502(c) claim, the court exercises its discretion per Penal Code 502(e)(1) not to award any fees to FusionStorm. FusionStorm never provided any persuasive evidence that Ms. Tenorio misused any of FusionStorm's information in a way that caused any harm. Moreover, the injunctive relief it sought and obtained was more prophylactic than remedial. Judge; Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

Motion For Attorneys Fees After Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Cross-defendant FusionStorm's motion for attorney's fees after judgment is denied. Although FusionStorm has neither waived nor is judicially estopped from seeking attorney's fees, it fails to identify a basis to obtain a fees award. A prevailing defendant on a FEHA claim is not entitled to an award of fees unless "unless the court finds the action was objectively without foundation when brought, or the plaintiff continued to litigate after it clearly became so." (Williams v. Chino Valley Independent Fire District (2015) 61 Cal. 4th 97, 115). The court's own review of the file in this case, including the papers filed on this motion, is insufficient to support a finding that Ms. Tenorio's FEHA claims were objectively without foundation when brought or she continued to litigate them after they clearly became so. Nor is FusionStorm entitled to fees per the fees provision in Ms. Tenorio's severance agreement because, by this lawsuit, FusionStorm did not recover any consideration paid to Ms. Tenorio per that agreement, which is the only basis for an award of contractual fees relied on by FusionStorm. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge; Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Plaintiff And Cross- Defendant Fusionstorms Notice Of Joint Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-23-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Atty. Allison Ross appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Jain P. Horn appearing for the Defendant.
RULING - The Matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff and cross-defendant FusionStorm's motion for summary judgment is granted. On FusionStorrm's complaint, it is entitled to judgment in its favor and against defendant and cross-complainant Renee Tenorio for a permanent injunction enjoining Ms. Tenorio from directly or indirectly using, disclosing or disseminating any non-public information that is contained in any document including an email that Ms. Tenorio prepared or received while she was employed at FusionStorm and was at any time maintained in a FusionStorm computer file. On Ms. Tenorio's sole remaining cause of action for breach of the severance agreement FusionStorm is entitled to judgment that Ms. Tenorio take nothing on that cause of action. Fairly read although not explicitly stated in its motion papers, FusionStorm has waived its requests for monetary relief in its complaint and seeks only permanent injunctive relief on its two causes of action for misappropriation of trade secrets and civil liability for violation of Penal Code 502. FusionStorm did not satisfy its summary motion burden of showing that Ms. Tenorio misappropriated any information that was the subject of reasonable efforts to maintain the secrecy of that information (Civil Code 3246.1(d)(2) and thus FusionStorm is not entitled to judgment in its favor on its trade secret claim. FusionStorm satisfied its summary motion burden of showing that Ms. Tenorio knowing accessed and without permission took non-public data from FusionStorm's computer system and that FusionStorm could possibly be harmed if Ms. Tenorio uses, discloses or disseminates that information. Because Ms. Tentoro did not submit any evidence creating a triable dispute on those issues, FusionStorm is entitled to injunctive relief on its section 502 claim. (Penal Code 502(c)(2) and (e)(1)). FusionStorm also satisfied its summary motion burden that Ms. Tenorio remains in possession of FusionStorm proprietary information and thus, per the first paragraph on page 4 of Ms. Tenorio's severance agreement, a condition precedent to FusionStorm's performance under that agreement has not occurred and FusionStorm is not liable to Ms. Tenorio for any non-performance by it under that agreement. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-26-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

CROSS DEFENDANT FUSIONSTORM DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT, as to the second and third causes of action in the first amended cross-complaint filed by Renee Tenorio is SUSTAINED with 20 days leave to amend as to both causes action. The allegations in the first amended cross-complaint are deficient to state claims for wrongful discharge or demotion in violation of public policy and discriminatory disparate treatment or disparate impact termination of employment. The allegations that Ms. Tenorio protested Fusionstorm's allegedly discriminatory reduction in force practices prior to and at the time of Ms. Tenorio's demotion are time barred because her DFEH charge was filed more than one year after the demotion occurred. (Gov't Code section 12960.) However, it appears that Ms. Tenorio is able to allege that she continued to question the RIF practices after her demotion and thus can likely plead a non-time barred wrongful termination in violation of public policy claim based on her post-demotion conduct that she alleges resulted in her termination. Ms. Tenorio's proposed second amended cross-complaint is likely sufficient to allege a claim that the termination of her employment was based on discriminatory disparate treatment of her as a member of a protected class. However, as currently alleged and proposed to be alleged, Ms. Tenorio's allegations are insufficient to state a disparate impact claim because there is no allegation of a facially neutral policy that disparately impacted members in Ms. Tenorio's protected class. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

Preliminary Injunction Hearing
Off Calendar Jan-09-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARK V. BOENNINGHAUSEN OF THE THOITS LAW, TEL. NO. 1-650-330-4706 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. IAN ANDERSON OF THE KAUFMAN DOLOWICH & VOLUCK, LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-926-7614 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff FusionStorm's motion for a preliminary injunction is granted. Defendant Renee Tenorio is enjoined, pending the entry of judgment in this case, from directly or indirectly using, disclosing or disseminating any files, information or data taken from FusionStorm. "A trial court deciding whether to issue a preliminary injunction weighs two interrelated factors-the likelihood the moving party will prevail on the merits at trial and the relative balance of interim harms that are likely to result from the granting or denial of preliminary injunctive relief." (County of Kern v. T.C.E.F., Inc. (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 301, 315.) "The trial court's determination must be guided by a 'mix' of the potential-merit and interim-harm factors; the greater the plaintiff's showing on one, the less must be shown on the other to support an injunction." (Butt v. State of California (1992) 4 Cal.4th 668, 678.) Both factors favor FusionStorm. FusionStorm has shown that it will likely prevail on its claim that Ms. Tenorio misappropriated FusionStorm's trade secrets. The files that Ms. Tenorio took form FusionStorm are a compilation of information pertaining to employees' health, compensation, surveys, wage garnishments, exit letters, performance reviews as well as vendor contracts. FusionStorm has shown that this information has independent economic value. For example, a competitor could use the information to identify and retain FusionStorm's top performing employees. FusionStorm has also shown it took reasonable efforts to maintain the secrecy of the information because the information was password protected, access was limited to a small number of employees, and Ms. Tenorio was required to sign a confidentiality agreement. FusionStorm has further shown Ms. Tenorio retained the information on two flash drives after signing a severance letter prohibiting her from doing so. Balancing of the interim harms favors FusionStorm since a denial of an injunction could result in substantial harm to FusionStorm and Ms. Tenorio would suffer no harm if it were granted. FusionStorm indicates it has a legal and moral obligation to maintain the privacy of its employees' personal information, employees would be harmed if their private health and financial information was disclosed, information could be used by competitors to try to hire away FusionStorm's employees, and vendor contracts and operational information may be used to unfairly compete with FusionStorm. Ms. Tenorio provided no argument or evidence that she would suffer harm should the preliminary injunction be granted. The court finds that, based on the facts presented on the motion, that there is a significant threat that Ms. Tenorio would misappropriate FusionStorm's trade secrets in the future absent granting the injunctive relief requested by FusionStorm. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/hk
